title: Rare Evo and Dev Gov Day 2026: Cardano Title Sponsorship

rationale.summary: Vote: NEEDS_MORE_INFO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (expired), critical information needed for a responsible directional vote is still missing. Decisive reason: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "The primary mission of this proposal is to strengthen and expand Cardano's global ecosystem presence through Rare Evo 2026 and Rare Dev Gov Day 2026, two established community-led initiatives that have become major coordination, onboarding, networking, gove..." though 7 review blocker(s) remain open. What would enable a directional vote: Provide: Deep research dossier is required for treasury actions before directional voting; Provide: Complete proposal summary, budget analysis, feasibility, risks, alternatives, and failure-mode sections. Residual uncertainty: Dossier completeness not confirmed for this treasury proposal. Confidence context: score 0.00; confidence 20.0%.
rationale.summary_raw: Directional voting is blocked until dossier quality gates pass.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Rare Evo and Dev Gov Day 2026: Cardano Title Sponsorship

Deliverables: The primary mission of this proposal is to strengthen and expand Cardano's global ecosystem presence through Rare Evo 2026 and Rare Dev Gov Day 2026, two established community-led initiatives that have become major coordination, onboarding, networking, gove..., This proposal would position the Cardano ecosystem as the title sponsor of the Rare Evo 2026 main stage and global livestream, while also supporting the second annual Rare Dev Gov Day, a dedicated Cardano-focused technical, governance, and community coordin..., The proposal is designed to create direct ecosystem value through: - Global Cardano brand visibility - Ecosystem onboarding and education - Governance participation - Developer and startup exposure - Enterprise and institutional outreach - Cross-chain indus..., Maintaining strong ecosystem visibility, participation, and coordination infrastructure during difficult market cycles is critical for long-term ecosystem resilience and growth.

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): The primary mission of this proposal is to strengthen and expand Cardano's global ecosystem presence through Rare Evo 2026 and Rare Dev Gov Day 2026, two established community-led initiatives that have become major coordination, onboarding, networking, gove...

Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): This proposal would position the Cardano ecosystem as the title sponsor of the Rare Evo 2026 main stage and global livestream, while also supporting the second annual Rare Dev Gov Day, a dedicated Cardano-focused technical, governance, and community coordin...

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): The proposal is designed to create direct ecosystem value through: - Global Cardano brand visibility - Ecosystem onboarding and education - Governance participation - Developer and startup exposure - Enterprise and institutional outreach - Cross-chain indus...

Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, low materiality): Maintaining strong ecosystem visibility, participation, and coordination infrastructure during difficult market cycles is critical for long-term ecosystem resilience and growth.

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): Rare Network has been active in the Cardano ecosystem since 2020, beginning with the launch of the RARE stake pool and evolving into one of the most consistent producers of Cardano-focused community building and ecosystem growth events, activations, and eco...

Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): Over the past half decade, the organization has built extensive operational infrastructure, industry relationships, and community trust through the successful execution of conferences, governance initiatives, side events, ecosystem showcases, and global blo...
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The primary mission of this proposal is to strengthen and expand Cardano's global ecosystem presence through Rare Evo 2026 and Rare Dev Gov Day 2026, two established community-led initiatives that have become major coordination, onboarding, networking, gove..." though 7 review blocker(s) remain open.
Strongest NO: an unresolved blocker (missing complete proposal summary) means costs or weak controls may outweigh the claimed benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

actionable_asks: Publish a line-item budget: what is being bought, at what unit cost.
State the cost against the benefit: what does this buy, per ada spent.
Describe the funding path beyond this grant, or say plainly that there isn't one.
Disclose the rate basis — headcount, FTE fraction, or day rate — so the price can be checked against the market.
Tie payment to defined deliverables, not to elapsed time or headcount.
State your AI-cost assumption: this work is cheaper to produce than it was, and the price should show it.
Gate disbursement on milestones that must be met before money moves.
Provide a clawback or refund path if the work is not delivered.
missing: verified ecosystem benefit or strategic necessity
cost comparables, budget proportionality, or sustainability evidence
delivery controls, milestones, assurance, or remedy path

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.benefit_formula: 18% critical infrastructure + 8% open source + 15% adoption + 12% ecosystem leverage + 10% strategic necessity + 10% prior delivery + 10% established service + 8% builder dependency + 5% low substitutability + 4% non-funding disruption risk
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.

flags.detail: No links to forum discussion or community feedback
Treasury withdrawal to a single address

treasury_capacity.caveat: Set by an Info governance action. Info actions change no ledger state and can NEVER be enacted -- they always expire -- so no formally ratified NCL exists or can exist. This is the strongest available public chain evidence: an anchor document byte-verified against its on-chain blake2b-256 hash, covering the current epoch, which won a majority of participating DRep stake. It is NOT enacted ledger state, and the majority may fall short of the supermajority other action types require. Published with every treasury rationale that relies on it.
